mentions.raw_name: The brother of the Archon of Tyrosh (unnamed)
Braavos — the big house with the red door
Ornate oil lanterns with pale blue glass panes
Mosaic depicting the Doom of Valyria
Daenerys Targaryen (Dany, Daenerys Stormborn, Princess of Dragonstone)
Illyrio Mopatis (Magister of the Free City of Pentos)
The Usurper's brother / Stannis Baratheon (referenced, unnamed)
Lord Lannister (referenced — sack of King's Landing)
Lord Stark (referenced — sack of King's Landing)
The brother of the Archon of Tyrosh (unnamed, present at feast)
Drogo's three bloodriders (unnamed, present at feast)
Khal Drogo's manse (Pentos, nine-towered, by the bay)
Westeros / the Sunset Kingdoms / Rhaesh Andahli
Braavos (the big house with the red door)
The Unsullied (guard at the gate wears their spiked bronze cap)
Drogo's khalasar (referenced — one hundred thousand men)
The Night's Watch (referenced — Jorah's poachers should have gone there)
The Lord of Light / R'hllor (Illyrio blesses Dany in his name; red priests sing and light night fires; red priest present at the feast; red priests promise to hold city walls)
The Faith of the Seven (the High Septon anointed Ser Jorah with seven oils — referenced)
Dothraki (horselords, braids, bells, copper skin, mustachios, scented oil, mounted warriors, slaves with golden collars, khalasar structure)
Valyrians / blood of old Valyria (golden blood, Targaryen heritage, sibling marriage tradition)
Andals (Rhaesh Andahli — what the Dothraki call Westeros)
First Men (in Viserys's title: King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men)
Pentoshi (magisters, archons, the Free City's laws nominally forbid slavery)
Tyroshi (Tyroshi slaver; sellswords from Tyrosh; brother of the Archon of Tyrosh)
Ibbenese (hairy men from the Port of Ibben)
Summer Islanders (lords with skin as black as ebony)
Deep plum silk gown (gift from Illyrio)
Borrowed sword (lent by Illyrio to Viserys)
Polished dragon skulls (referenced — in the throne room)
Mosaic of colored glass depicting the Doom of Valyria
Ornate oil lanterns with pale blue glass panes
"The fire is in our blood" — Targaryen belief about heat resistance (Dany does not flinch from scalding water)
"Waking the dragon" — Viserys's term for his rage; also evokes dragon imagery of House Targaryen
Robert's Rebellion / the Usurper's war (referenced — Rhaegar's death at the Trident, sack of King's Landing, flight to Dragonstone, fall of the Targaryen dynasty)
King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men (claimed by Viserys)
Lord of the Seven Kingdoms (claimed by Viserys)
Protector of the Realm (claimed by Viserys)
Archon (title in Tyrosh — brother of the Archon present)
Dragonbone (trade good dealt by Illyrio; also material of Viserys's brooch)
Spiceflower perfume of the Dothraki plains
